items.title: Protesters clash with federal authorities during immigration raid in California
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Border Patrol personnel in riot gear and gas masks stood guard outside an industrial park in the city of Paramount, deploying tear gas as bystanders and protesters gathered after U.S. immigration authorities extended activity in the Los Angeles area Saturday. 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: US Army tanks and vehicles arrive in Washington for military parade
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>U.S. Army tanks, Bradley fighting vehicles, Paladin howitzers and other military equipment arrived by train to the nation's capital Saturday to take part in the military parade for the service's 250th birthday celebration on June 14.

items.title: Justice on artistry, awards and playing the long game | AP interview
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>French electronic duo Justice — comprised of Xavier de Rosnay and Gaspard Augé —  sat down with AP entertainment journalist Liam McEwan to talk about latest album “Hyperdrama,” the art of letting go in the studio, and why their live shows are carefully crafted for maximum impact — even for audiences unfamiliar with their work. The three-time Grammy winners open up about resisting pressure to release music on a fixed timeline, their recent collaboration with The Weeknd, their creative process, and embracing an evolving fanbase. #justice #music</article>

items.title: Boy pulled alive from rubble following Israeli strike on Gaza City, but children among dead
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>At least six people including children were killed in an Israeli strike in Gaza City on Saturday, according to Shifa and Al Ahli hospital officials.
One child was rescued alive and carried to paramedics by his father.
The dead bodies of a boy and girl were also recovered during the search, and placed in ambulances.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Russian drones and missiles target Ukraine's eastern city of Kharkiv, killing 3, officials say
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The barrage — the latest in near daily widescale attacks — included aerial glide bombs that have become part of a fierce Russian onslaught on Ukraine in the three-year war.

items.title: Ukraine security service says video shows drone's flight in attack on Russian air bases
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Ukraine's security service has released more footage which it said showed its attack on Russian war planes at one of the air bases targeted last Sunday.
The footage showed a drone taking off from a truck's roof and swooping low over parked aircraft, as well as planes burning at the Russian military Belaya airbase.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: Ángela Aguilar is reintroducing herself with 'Nadie Se Va Como Llegó'
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Ángela Aguilar steps into a new era with “Nadie Se Va Como Llegó." The 21-year-old grew up in the limelight as the daughter of Grammy-winning recording artist Pepe Aguilar and granddaughter of renowned Mexican icons Flor Silvestre and Antonio Aguilar.
#angelaaguilar #nadiesevacomollego #shorts</article>

items.title: Colombian authorities carry out rescues after rafts and kayak capsize in deadly flash flooding
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Several rafts and a kayak capsized Friday on the Güejar River in central Colombia due to a sudden flood that left at least four people dead, the National Unit for Disaster Risk Management said.

items.title: Pope Leo XIV receives the President of Argentina, Javier Milei, at the Vatican
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Pope Leo XIV met Argentine President Javier Milei on Saturday in the Vatican Apostolic Palace.  According to a note from the Vatican press office, Pope Leo  and Milei discussed "matters of common interest, such as social-economic progress, the fight against poverty, and the commitment to social cohesion," and particular attention was paid to ongoing conflicts "noting the importance of an urgent commitment in support of peace."

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: A U.S. territory's colonial history emerges in state disputes over voting and citizenship
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A criminal case in Alaska involving nearly a dozen people born in American Samoa is highlighting concerns about birthright citizenship and voting by people who are not U.S. citizens. (AP Video by Mark Thiessen, Claire Rush, Manuel Valdes)

items.title: Catholic pilgrims gather in El Rocío in Spain to venerate an icon of the Virgin Mary, AP explains
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Tens of thousands of pilgrims have descended on the tiny Spanish village of El Rocío to take part in riotously colorful and ancient festival, la Romería del Rocío, or the Rocío virgin pilgrimage.
